title: quercus aliena
intro: Quercus aliena, also known as Japanese evergreen oak, is a species of evergreen tree or shrub native to East Asia. The bark, leaves, and roots of Quercus aliena have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Quercus aliena include reducing inflammation and improving immune function, as well as preventing the formation of blood clots and lowering cholesterol levels. Quercus aliena can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal purposes, and it is also used in traditional East Asian cooking as a spice.
